positioning: Educational scenario model, not a claimed Waymo P&L or investment forecast.

annual_total_vehicle_miles_per_vehicle.methodology_note: Held constant in v0.1 to keep the public interface focused. It may become an advanced variable later.

residual_value_rate.unit: share of initial vehicle and AV-kit capex
residual_value_rate.methodology_note: Represents conservative component, battery and recovery value. It does not assume a mature whole-vehicle robotaxi resale market.

net_revenue_per_paid_mile.label: Net revenue per paid mile
net_revenue_per_paid_mile.range_basis: Modelled from a gross-fare range and 10% to 20% combined transaction, access, distribution, promotion and service-recovery leakage. Direct Waymo net-revenue disclosure is not available.
net_revenue_per_paid_mile.justification: The tool uses net revenue rather than gross fare so readers can test the economics without separately modelling every tax, partner fee, discount and payment charge.
net_revenue_per_paid_mile.methodology_note: Revenue retained by the robotaxi operating system after external leakage. It is not EBITDA and excludes no operating costs shown elsewhere.

paid_mile_ratio.label: Paid-mile utilisation
paid_mile_ratio.range_basis: The base reflects a Waymo-like observed passenger-onboard share. Low and high cases represent early or fragmented operation versus a dense and well-balanced mature network.
paid_mile_ratio.justification: Every empty mile still carries depreciation, energy, maintenance, insurance and local operating cost. Utilisation therefore multiplies almost the entire direct cost stack.
paid_mile_ratio.methodology_note: Paid vehicle miles divided by total vehicle miles. This is not passenger occupancy and not the share of hours a vehicle is active.

vehicle_av_capex.label: Vehicle and AV-kit cost
vehicle_av_capex.range_basis: Current-generation premium-EV plus autonomous-integration scenario range. The exact Waymo all-in cost is not publicly disclosed.
vehicle_av_capex.justification: Vehicle cost matters through depreciation, but its effect depends equally on useful lifetime miles, residual value and paid-mile utilisation.
vehicle_av_capex.methodology_note: Includes the base vehicle and installed autonomous-driving hardware. It excludes depot infrastructure and central platform development.

vehicle_lifetime_miles.label: Vehicle lifetime miles
vehicle_lifetime_miles.range_basis: Scenario range reflecting high-mileage commercial use, accelerated hardware obsolescence and uncertain robotaxi residual markets.
vehicle_lifetime_miles.justification: A cheaper vehicle does not necessarily improve economics if high duty cycles, battery wear or AV-hardware obsolescence shorten its productive life.
vehicle_lifetime_miles.methodology_note: Lifetime total vehicle miles before retirement or major economic replacement, not paid miles.

direct_running_cost_per_total_mile.label: Direct running cost
direct_running_cost_per_total_mile.range_basis: Consolidated scenario for energy, routine EV maintenance, AV-specific maintenance and insurance/liability reserve. The range reflects uncertainty in AV repair frequency and insurance translation.
direct_running_cost_per_total_mile.justification: This combines the direct exposure-mile costs that move with vehicle use while keeping depreciation and local fleet operations separate.
direct_running_cost_per_total_mile.methodology_note: Includes electricity, routine maintenance, AV-specific maintenance and insurance/liability reserve. Excludes depreciation, depot operations and fixed city cost.

local_fleet_ops_cost_per_total_mile.label: Local fleet-operations cost
local_fleet_ops_cost_per_total_mile.range_basis: Scenario range for depot readiness, charging infrastructure, remote assistance, rider support and field response after overlap controls.
local_fleet_ops_cost_per_total_mile.justification: Removing the driver shifts labour and infrastructure into depots, control centres and field operations. Local density determines how efficiently those functions are shared.
local_fleet_ops_cost_per_total_mile.methodology_note: Variable local cost per total vehicle mile. It excludes the separate annual city cost so density effects remain visible.

active_vehicles_per_city.label: Active vehicles per city
active_vehicles_per_city.range_basis: Scenario range designed to test local operating density. Public operator fleet counts are incomplete and may include test, reserve and inactive vehicles.
active_vehicles_per_city.justification: Fleet density dilutes recurring city costs and, together with the number of cities, determines how many paid miles absorb the central platform.
active_vehicles_per_city.methodology_note: Active commercial vehicles in an average city. Total fleet is derived as vehicles per city multiplied by the number of cities.

number_of_cities.label: Number of active cities
number_of_cities.range_basis: Scenario architecture rather than a claim about any operator's exact commercial footprint. Each city is assumed to have the same average active fleet and annual city cost.
number_of_cities.justification: More cities expand the paid-mile denominator but also add local fixed cost. Whether expansion creates leverage depends on fleet density and platform reuse.
number_of_cities.methodology_note: Active commercial city-equivalents. A large metropolitan service area and a small city are treated as equal units in v0.1, which is a deliberate simplification.

solver.reason: Direction can change depending on city density and annual city cost.

annual_platform_cost.label: Annual platform cost
annual_platform_cost.range_basis: Transparent allocated scenario pool for core autonomy, AI-production activity, simulation, data and software assurance. It is not derived from Alphabet Other Bets as a Waymo P&L.
annual_platform_cost.justification: The platform layer remains a continuing production system. Its economic test is whether paid miles and validated service coverage grow faster than the burden required to produce them.
annual_platform_cost.methodology_note: Annual enterprise cost shared across all active cities and vehicles. Regional and city-specific costs are excluded and represented elsewhere.

solver.reason: Advanced variable; can be solved analytically in methodology outputs if required.

annual_city_cost_per_city.label: Annual city and launch burden
annual_city_cost_per_city.range_basis: Combines recurring city overhead with annualised launch and ODD-validation cost. Public company-level city-launch economics are not disclosed.
annual_city_cost_per_city.justification: Every new city reopens part of the cost stack. The key replication test is whether this annualised burden falls as launch playbooks, validation evidence and infrastructure partnerships become reusable.
annual_city_cost_per_city.methodology_note: Annual city-equivalent cost including recurring local overhead and amortised launch burden. Variable depot operations are excluded.

solver.reason: Advanced variable; can be solved analytically in methodology outputs if required.
